prompt:
Fix the Hyprland window rule that hides the Google Meet screen-sharing overlay. PR #464 was merged but the rule doesn't work because it matches the wrong window class.

## Problem
In home/hyprland.nix around line 183, the rule is:
  "match:class ^(google-chrome)$, match:float yes, match:title .*is sharing your screen.*, workspace special:trash silent"

But the actual overlay window has an EMPTY class (""), not "google-chrome". Proof from hyprctl clients -j:
  title: "meet.google.com is sharing your screen."
  class: ""
  floating: true

## Fix
Change line 183 to drop the class matcher:
  "match:float yes, match:title .*is sharing your screen.*, workspace special:trash silent"

## Waybar indicator
The PR also added a custom/screenshare waybar module in home/waybar.nix that detects the overlay via hyprctl clients. Verify that hyprctl clients still shows windows on special:trash workspaces. If it does, the indicator will keep working. If not, switch detection to checking for pipewire screen capture (e.g. pw-cli or /proc/*/fd pointing to pipewire screencast).

## Constraints
- System is Hyprland 0.54.3-b
- Run any pre-commit hooks or checks the repo has
- Keep the change minimal — only fix what's broken
